channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announces his resignation
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau has announced his resignation, telling the nation that “internal battles” mean he “cannot be the best option” in the next election. Trudeau said Monday he has asked the president of his Liberal Party to begin the process to select a new leader.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump pitches 'merged' US, Canada after Trudeau resignation announcement
item.description: President-elect Trump pitched the idea of a “merged” United States and Canada after Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announced his resignation, noting there would be no tariffs if the two countries were one. “If Canada merged with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Venezuelan opposition leader who claims to have defeated Maduro meets Biden at White House
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — The Venezuelan opposition leader who claims to have defeated President Nicolás Maduro in last year’s presidential election met Monday with U.S. President Joe Biden.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and REGINA GARCIA CANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada’s Trudeau resigns ahead of 2025 elections
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau resigned Monday, ahead of general elections later this year in which the ruling Liberal Party is polling badly and amid tensions with President-elect Trump, who has threatened steep tariffs on the country over border policies.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: What to know about the siege outside South Korea's presidential compound
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — A standoff between rival government forces outside the presidential compound in South Korea has been a startling development, even for observers used to the country's famously rough and tumble politics.
item.dc:creator: FOSTER KLUG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean anti-corruption agency and police debate tougher measures to detain impeached president
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s anti-corruption agency and police debated on Monday more forceful measures to detain impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol after their previous attempt was blocked by the presidential security service last week.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Austrian far right gets mandate to try to lead a government for the first time since World War II
item.description: The leader of Austria’s Freedom Party received a mandate Monday to form a new government, which would be the first headed by the far right since World War II if he succeeds.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russia claims it captured another town in eastern Ukraine but Kyiv's troops say the fight continues
item.description: Russia’s Defense Ministry is claiming that its troops in eastern Ukraine have captured the town of Kurakhove after a monthslong battle.
item.dc:creator: Illia Novikov, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: South Korea says North Korea fired a ballistic missile into the sea
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea on Monday fired a ballistic missile that flew 1,100 kilometers (685 miles) before landing in waters between the Korean Peninsula and Japan, South Korea’s military said, extending its weapons testing weeks before Donald Trump returns as U.S. president.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau to announce resignation: Reports
item.description: It remains unclear whether Justin Trudeau will leave immediately or stay on as prime minister until a new leader is selected, the report added.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Heavy snow brings widespread disruption across the UK and Germany
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Heavy snow and freezing rain brought widespread disruption across Europe on Sunday, particularly in the U.K. and Germany, with several major airports forced to suspend flights. With the weather set to stay inclement on Sunday in the U.K.
item.dc:creator: PAN PYLAS and MONIKA SCISLOWSKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Shigemi Fukahori, who survived Nagasaki bombing and spent his life advocating for peace, dies at 93
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Shigemi Fukahori, a survivor of the 1945 Nagasaki atomic bombing, who devoted his life to advocating for peace and campaigning against nuclear weapons, has died. He was 93. Fukahori died at a hospital in Nagasaki, southwestern Japan, on Jan.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Italy's Meloni makes surprise Mar-a-Lago visit to meet with Trump
item.description: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni made a surprise visit to Mar-a-Lago to meet with President-elect Trump on Saturday. They were joined by a number of Trump’s expected nominees for his forthcoming administration, according to a pool report, including Sen. Marco Rubio (R-Fla.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Musk says UK's Farage should not be Reform party leader
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk said Sunday that British politician Nigel Farage should not be the Reform U.K. party’s leader. “The Reform Party needs a new leader,” Musk said in a post on the social platform X, which he owns. “Farage doesn’t have what it takes.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Ukraine presses attacks in Russia's Kursk region
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — President Volodymyr Zelenskyy says he will urge allies to boost Ukraine’s air defenses at a meeting this week in Germany, while both sides said Kyiv’s forces pressed new attacks Sunday in Russia’s Kursk region.
item.dc:creator: VOLODYMYR YURCHUK and ELISE MORTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Speculation grows that Austrian far right leader Herbert Kickl will be asked to form a government
item.description: VIENNA (AP) — Austrian President Alexander Van der Bellen on Sunday announced that he would meet with far-right politician Herbert Kickl as speculation grows that he will ask the Freedom Party leader to form a government.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANIE LIECHTENSTEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korean protesters call for President Yoon’s arrest as deadline nears
item.description: Protesters in South Korea are calling for President Yoon Suk Yeol to be arrested as a warrant expires at midnight on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Trump announces Middle East appointment: Hopefully she's learned her lesson
item.description: President-elect Trump tapped Morgan Ortagus, a former State Department spokesperson, to serve as deputy special presidential envoy for Middle East peace in his next administration. Trump noted in his announcement that Ortagus previously was critical of him.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Greenland leader calls for independence from Denmark as Trump enters
item.description: Greenland Prime Minister Múte Egede announced a desire to obtain independence from Denmark in the wake of President-elect Trump’s comments about buying the island territory.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: One cigarette can decrease life expectancy by 20 minutes: Data
item.description: In 2000, data published in The BMJ estimated one cigarette would cost a person 11 minutes of their life.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: At least 30 people killed by Israeli strikes in Gaza
item.description: At least 30 people, including children, were killed in Gaza by Israeli strikes overnight and into Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: ISIS increasingly unopposed following US withdrawal from Afghanistan, collapse of Syria
item.description: The Islamic State is seeing less resistance in areas of major security concern following the U.S. withdrawal from Afghanistan, the fall of the Assad regime in Syria and potential troop drawdown in Iraq.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korea’s impeached president avoids arrest attempt after hourslong standoff
item.description: Impeached South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol avoided an arrest attempt Friday after an hourslong standoff between anti-corruption investigators and presidential security.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope calls for commitment to protect life as he doubles down on abortion in New Year's Day message
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis ushered in the New Year with a renewed appeal for the faithful to reject abortion, calling for a “firm commitment” to protect and respect life from conception to natural death. Francis, 88, celebrated a New Year’s Day Mass in St.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: German chancellor takes veiled swipe at Elon Musk in New Year's address
item.description: Scholz's comments come days after the German government accused the billionaire of trying to influence its February election.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, NewsNation

item.title: South Korea air crash investigators extract black box data as grieving families mourn the victims
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Grieving relatives of the victims of the South Korea plane crash gathered at the site to pay respects to their loved ones on New Year’s Day, as officials said they've extracted data from one of the retrieved black boxes to find the exact cause of the crash.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israeli strikes kill 12 in Gaza as war grinds into the new year
item.description: Israeli strikes killed at least 12 Palestinians in the Gaza Strip, mostly women and children, officials said.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation
